sessions.emptyState: No sessions yet. Start a new one to begin tracking your place in line.
sessions.deleteConfirm: Delete this session? This cannot be undone.

newSession.title: New Session
newSession.nameTooltip: Give your session a name so you can find it later.
newSession.currentNumTooltip: Look at the display board or ask — what number are they serving right now?
newSession.observedAtHint: Change this if you noted the number earlier but are entering it now.

onboarding.step1: Take a ticket and note your ticket number
onboarding.step2: Check what number is currently being served
onboarding.step3: Tap “+ New Queue Session” and enter both numbers
onboarding.step4: Each time a new number is called, log it — the app will predict your wait. Missed a few? No problem — just log the current number and predictions will continue

storageWarning: Your sessions are stored in this browser only. Clearing your site data or browser storage will erase all sessions.

tracker.numbersAhead: {n} number ahead of you. | {n} numbers ahead of you.
tracker.waitingHint: Log one more call to start predicting.

callLog.title: Log a number call
callLog.tooltip: When a new number is called, type it here and tap Log. The more you log, the better the prediction gets.
callLog.timeAutoFill: Time field auto-fills to now — edit it to log a past call
callLog.historyHint: Click any row to edit · ✕ to delete

help.title: Help
help.intro: Queue Watch helps you estimate how long you'll wait in a numbered queue. Enter your ticket number, note which number is currently being served, and log each new number as it's called. The app calculates the average service rate and predicts when your number will be reached.
help.step1: Create a session — give it a name (like “DMV” or “Bakery”) and enter your ticket number along with the current number being served.
help.step2: Each time a new number is called, open the session and log the number. The timestamp is recorded automatically. If you miss a few numbers, don’t worry — just log the current number and the app will continue to predict your wait.
help.step3: After two or more logs, Queue Watch computes the average rate (seconds per number) and estimates your remaining wait time and ETA.
help.step4: A trend indicator shows whether the queue is speeding up, slowing down, or holding steady compared to the overall average.
help.tip1: The more data points you log, the more accurate the prediction becomes.
help.tip2: If you missed a call, you can edit the time field when logging to backdate it.
help.tip3: Click any row in the call history to correct a mistake.
help.tip4: Use the 24h / 12h toggle (top-right of the tracker) to switch time format.
help.predictionsBody: The app calculates the average rate at which numbers are being called based on all your logged data points. It divides the numbers remaining by that rate to estimate your wait time and ETA. With three or more logs, it also compares the most recent rate to the overall average to show a trend — whether the queue is speeding up, slowing down, or holding steady. The more numbers you log, the more accurate the prediction becomes.
help.privacyBody: All your data is stored locally in your browser using localStorage. Nothing is sent to any server. No account is needed.
help.privacyWarning: Clearing your browser’s site data or storage will permanently erase all your saved sessions. There is no way to recover them.
help.multipleBody: You can track multiple queues at once — for example, if you’re waiting at two different counters. Each session is independent and can be resumed at any time from the home screen.
help.footer: Built with Vue + TypeScript + Claude Code

about.title: About
about.body: This application was conceived of while waiting in the Farmácia Alto Custo — Unidade Várzea do Carmo. Much love and respect for the team at Várzea do Carmo, SPDM in São Paulo, healthcare providers all over Brazil, and for SUS!
about.imageAlt: Eu Defendo o SUS — Sistema Único de Saúde
